Human Brain Mapping Laboratory

We study how brain dynamics give rise to different cognitive and autonomic functions using intracranial EEG (iEEG, ECoG) and electrical brain stimulation in humans. We are part of The Institute of Bioelectronic Medicine at The Feinstein Institutes for Medical Research in the Northwell Health System in New York.

Previous Next

Recruitment Open: Clinical Trial: Low-Intensity Ultrasonic Brain Stimulation for Investigational Treatment of Refractory Fibromyalgia

We aim to map and modulate functional and pathological networks in the human brain, by working with patients with epilepsy who have electrodes implanted in their brains in preparation for surgery to treat their seizures.

Our group works alongside the Epilepsy Monitoring Unit at NorthShore University Hospital and other epilepsy centers in the Northwell System.

News

April 2026

Serdar's paper on minimally invasive e-stim during speech perception got accepted in Hearing Research. Congrats Serdar and all co-authors!

March 2026

Akash successfully defended his thesis! Congratulations Dr. Mishra!

Sabina got promoted to Assistant Investigator. So well deserved!

September 2025

Jules Granget joined the lab as a postdoc with Jose! Welcome!!

September 2025

Serdar's speech entrainment paper got accepted in Progress in Neurobiology. Congrats Serdar and all co-authors!

August 2025

Akash's HFO motifs paper is published in Science Advances. Congrats Akash and all co-authors!

June 2025

Jessica Weber joined the team as a RA!! Welcome!
